## [00:00 - 03:00]

Hey, this is Lane with the Mastermind team and today we have a short video tutorial on how to create your product thumbnail in Canvas. So whether you're creating a coaching program, a mastermind or a course, you're going to use this thumbnail on your actual product pages and also on your sales page. And there might be other places you would want to use it as well. So it's really a versatile graphic to create and it's very simple using Canvas ready-made templates. So what you want before we get started is the name of your program and also your free Canvas account. And you can create that at canvacanva.com. So I have already opened up the Canvas window and logged in. And now I'm going to go to the search function and let's say I've got a coaching program. So I want to create a coaching thumbnail. Let's call it. And it will give us all sorts of templates we can choose from. So look at all these already that we can choose from. We can filter these over here by clicking on this button. And I'm going to go down and filter just to look at the free options because I don't want to pay for this graphic. So here are the options I can choose from. So let's say my coaching program is on public speaking. So I want to choose something that I can easily adapt and make it and I want it to be suitable for my audience. So let's say this is a professional audience. So I don't want to pick something that's too casual or too childish in terms of it's for preschoolers or something like that. And I think for public speaking, let's take this one right here. I really like this because she's got the microphone she's working on and I won't even have to change the image. Once we open this template, we can change anything about it that we want. We can change the background image. We can change the text. We can change colors. For this right here, I love that she already looks like she's a public speaker. So we're going to choose this one. It's going to open it up and I'm going to click on customize this template. So once I open that image up, when I hover over different elements of the image with my mouse, you can see the highlight in purple. When it shows up as purple, that means I can edit it. So I can edit any of this text here. I could also change out the background image if I wanted to. I don't want to do that, so I'm going to leave that as is. But I'm going to change the name here and I'm going to play with the colors as well. So let's say the name of my coaching program is Earn Money Speaking. Not the best name in the world, but it's just something we're playing with. So I'm going to put all that in right here. And then you can see that speaking is too large for that text box. So I have two choices. I can make the font size smaller by highlighting it and going up here and reducing the font size till it all goes on one line. Or if I wanted to, I could keep the font size the same and I could just drag this box to

make the box bigger. So I have two choices there and I'm going to go with this right here. So we have Earn Money Speaking. I don't want people to subscribe. I want people to actually purchase this. So now we've got our image. It's done. If we wanted to, we could change the colors here. So all I do is double click on the text. Then I go up here and hover on the A and you'll see it says text color. I click on that and I can choose different colors. I can choose anything down here. It gives me some to choose from. I can even go up and choose my own color up here by using this selector. Or I can pick the eyedropper and let's say I want it to match the color of the loudspeaker there and make it red. Then I just do that. I just use the eyedropper and it changes the color. So I want to change the rest of this and make it red as well. So I double click on money. Go up here to the text color.
